name: Unified Content References
description: Unified notation for referencing any form of content - data, files, areas, and more

content.content:
# Unified Content References

MeshWeaver provides a unified notation for referencing any form of content. Whether you need to embed data, include file content, display layout areas, or select AI agents and models, the syntax follows a consistent pattern:

```
@{pathToAddress}/{pathType}:{pathToObject}
```

Where:
- `{pathToAddress}` is the address (e.g., `app/Documentation`, `ACME`, `graph/story1`)
- `{pathType}` specifies the type of content to access
- `{pathToObject}` is the specific path to the object

## Path Types

The `{pathType}` determines how the content is fetched and rendered:

- `data` - Fetches data entities from collections
- `content` - Fetches file content from content collections and renders based on mime type
- `collection` - Fetches content collection configuration
- `area` - Displays a layout area (this is the default when no path type is specified)
- `type` - Accesses NodeType configuration
- `schema` - Accesses JSON schema for types
- `model` - Accesses data model information

Special prefixes for AI interactions:
- `agent/` - Selects an AI agent for chat interactions
- `model/` - Selects an AI model for chat interactions

For paths containing spaces, use quotes: `@"app/Docs/content:My Report.pdf"`

---

## Content References

Content references embed file content directly in your markdown. The format is:

```
@{address}/content:{collection}/{path}
```

The `content:` prefix tells MeshWeaver to fetch file content from a content collection. The file is then rendered based on its MIME type.

### Examples

**Embedding an image:**
```
@app/Documentation/content:Documentation/images/meshbros.png
```

**Embedding a markdown document:**
```
@app/Documentation/content:Documentation/embedded.md
```

**Accessing files from node instances:**
```
@ACME/content:attachments/test.txt
```

This fetches the `test.txt` file from the `attachments` collection of the ACME organization.

---

## Layout Area References

Layout areas display interactive components. The format is:

```
@{address}/{areaName}
```

Since `area` is the default path type, you don't need to specify it explicitly. However, you can also use:

```
@{address}/area:{areaName}
```

### Examples

**Calculator component:**
```
@app/Documentation/Calculator
```

**Counter component:**
```
@app/Documentation/Counter
```

**Progress indicators:**
```
@app/Documentation/Progress
```

---

## Data References

Data references fetch data entities from data collections. The format is:

```
@{address}/data:{collection}[/{entityId}]
```

The `data:` prefix tells MeshWeaver to fetch data from a data collection. You can optionally specify an entity ID.

### Examples

**Fetch all entities from a collection:**
```
@graph/data:stories
```

This retrieves all stories from the `stories` data collection in the graph hub.

**Fetch a specific entity:**
```
@graph/data:stories/story1
```

This retrieves the story with ID `story1` from the `stories` collection.

---

## Collection References

Collection references fetch content collection configuration. The format is:

```
@{address}/collection:{collectionName}
```

### Example

**Get collection configuration:**
```
@ACME/collection:attachments
```

This retrieves the configuration for the `attachments` content collection from the ACME organization, including information like the collection's base path and source type.

---

## Type References

Type references access NodeType configuration code. The format is:

```
@{address}/type
```

### Example

**Access NodeType configuration:**
```
@type/story/type
```

This retrieves the `CodeConfiguration` for the Story NodeType, which includes the C# record definition.

---

## Schema References

Schema references access JSON schema definitions for types. The format is:

```
@{address}/schema[/{typeName}]
```

### Examples

**Get type schema:**
```
@graph/schema/Story
```

This retrieves the JSON schema definition for the Story type.

**Get default hub schema:**
```
@graph/schema
```

---

## Model References

Model references access data model information, returning descriptions of all registered types. The format is:

```
@{address}/model
```

### Example

**Get data model:**
```
@graph/model
```

This retrieves type descriptions (name, display name, description) for all registered types in the graph hub.

---

## Agent References

Agent references allow you to select a specific AI agent for chat interactions. The format is:

```
@agent/AgentName
```

Agents are specialized AI assistants configured for specific tasks or domains.

### Examples

**Select an agent:**
```
@agent/Documentation
```

**Combine with a prompt:**
```
@agent/RiskImportAgent import Microsoft.xlsx
```

Agents can also be selected automatically based on navigation context.

---

## AI Model Selection

Model references allow you to select a specific AI model for chat interactions. The format is:

```
@model/ModelName
```

### Example

**Select a model:**
```
@model/claude-3-5-sonnet
```

Model names can contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots.

---

## Slash Commands

In addition to @ references, you can use slash commands:

- `/agent AgentName` - Switch to the specified agent
- `/model ModelName` - Switch to the specified model
- `/help` - Show available commands

### Examples

**Switch agent:**
```
/agent @agent/RiskImportAgent
```

**Switch model:**
```
/model @model/claude-haiku-4-5
```

---

## Summary

The unified reference syntax provides a consistent way to access all forms of content in MeshWeaver:

| Path Type | Format | Example |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Content | `@{address}/content:{collection}/{path}` | `@ACME/content:attachments/logo.png` |
| Data | `@{address}/data:{collection}[/{id}]` | `@graph/data:stories/story1` |
| Collection | `@{address}/collection:{name}` | `@ACME/collection:attachments` |
| Area | `@{address}/{areaName}` | `@app/Documentation/Calculator` |
| Type | `@{address}/type` | `@type/story/type` |
| Schema | `@{address}/schema[/{type}]` | `@graph/schema/Story` |
| Model | `@{address}/model` | `@graph/model` |
| Agent | `@agent/{name}` | `@agent/Documentation` |
| AI Model | `@model/{name}` | `@model/claude-3-5-sonnet` |
